svg.aria-label: Bead-maze of 25 detector-recovered three-body choreographies: 21 strict single-curve and 4 relative (2pi/3 rotation). A detector and literature-count reconciliation, not theorem evidence.
svg.metadata: Classes read from results/isotrophy/m3eq1-sigma3-precondition-fixed-inverse-orientation-25/residuals.csv; orbits integrated via scripts/isotrophy_workbench.py from the supplementary-A mirror. G.2 = detector + literature-count reconciliation (21 strict single-curve + 4 relative = 25 gauge-invariant, vs 21 in the literature). NOT theorem evidence. The v0.2 daughter-count test was retired at K1 as the equivariance-only null (K_facet=0). K_facet=0 does NOT mean no piano-trios; the 4 relatives are outside the strict single-curve convention, not literature mistakes. See internal/anniversary/isotrophy_handoff_note.md.
svg.text: Three-body choreographies, sorted by the wire they ride
σ₃ detector vs the Li–Liao 10,059-orbit catalog: 21 strict single-curve + 4 relative (↻ 2π/3) = 25 gauge-invariant, reconciling the literature’s 21 — a detector win, not theorem evidence
■ strict single-curve (21) ■ relative, 2π/3 global rotation (4) — outside the strict convention, not literature errors

What this is — a detector and literature-count win. The σ₃ workbench recovered the 21 strict equal-mass choreographies and cleanly split off 4 relative ones (a 2π/3 global rotation), explaining why a gauge-invariant gate sees 25 where the literature says 21. It is not theorem evidence.

The proposed daughter-count theorem test was retired at its cheap K1 precheck: under v0.2 it reduced to the equivariance-only null (K_facet = 0; generically Z₃ ∩ Z₂ = {e}). The cheap falsifier ran before the multi-hour sweep.

K_facet = 0 does not mean “no piano-trios,” and the 4 relatives are outside the strict single-curve convention, not literature errors. This is a receipt for the discipline: publish the boundary, do not patch the operator. sundog_v_isotrophy.md · isotrophy_handoff_note.md
